steps.title: Identify the conclusion
steps.content: Valli and Brenner concluded that sediment influx in the Alpine streams may have been spatially diffuse. That means sediment was entering the streams from many distributed locations rather than mainly from one primary source.

steps.title: Find distributed sediment sources
steps.content: Choice B concerns sediment type, not where sediment enters. Choice C emphasizes a common source. Choice D puts sediment-carrying tributaries downstream of the sampling sites, so they would not explain the samples. Bank collapses at multiple upstream points would spread sediment input across the streams. The answer is A.
